javadoc_before:
/**
 * A native int set where one value is reserved to mean "EMPTY"
 *
 * @lucene.internal
 */
javadoc_after:
/** * A native int hash-based set where one value is reserved to mean "EMPTY" internally. The space overhead is fairly low * as there is only one power-of-two sized int[] to hold the values. The set is re-hashed when adding a value that * would make it >= 75% full. Consider extending and over-riding {@link #hash(int)} if the values might be poor * hash keys; Lucene docids should be fine. * The internal fields are exposed publicly to enable more efficient use at the expense of better O-O principles. *

* To iterate over the integers held in this set, simply use code like this: * 

 * SentinelIntSet set = ...
 * for (int v : set.keys) {
 *   if (v == set.emptyVal)
 *     continue;
 *   //use v...
 * }
* * @lucene.internal */
